description: An IT manager at a health informatics company is modernizing the Oracle HR database archival strategy. The JOB_HISTORY table contains historical employment records but lacks partitioning, making range-based purges expensive. Modernize the archival strategy by creating a range-partitioned table named EMPLOYEE_HISTORY_ARCHIVE partitioned by END_DATE with four decade partitions (before 1990, 1990s, 2000s, and 2010+). Migrate all rows from JOB_HISTORY into this new table and create bitmap indexes on the JOB_ID and DEPARTMENT_ID columns. Additionally, create a materialized view named MV_DEPT_TURNOVER showing per-department job change counts from the archive. Save a partition analysis report showing row counts per partition to /home/ga/Desktop/archive_analysis.txt. All objects must be valid and the archive must contain all original JOB_HISTORY rows.
